items.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.summary: OpenAI introduces Deployment Simulation, a method to predict AI model behavior before deployment using real conversation data to improve safety and evaluation accuracy.

items.title: Introducing GPT-Live
items.summary: A new generation of voice models for natural human-AI interaction, now powering ChatGPT Voice.

items.title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI
items.summary: HP Inc. scales its OpenAI Frontier partnership to deploy AI across customer experiences, software development, and enterprise operations.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly running a "coordinated campaign" to steal trade secrets through poached employees
items.summary: Apple is suing OpenAI over systematic employee poaching and the alleged theft of trade secrets tied to unreleased products. According to the complaint, more than 400 ex-Apple employees now work at OpenAI, including former iPhone design chief Tang Tan. The lawsuit hits OpenAI right as it's building out its own hardware division, with its first product not expected to ship until 2027 at the earliest....

items.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol autonomously post-trained the smaller Luna model with a "fairly underspecified prompt"
items.summary: According to OpenAI, GPT-5.6 Sol independently fine-tuned the smaller Luna model, triggered by a single "fairly under-specified prompt." In OpenAI's internal RSI benchmark for recursive self-improvement, Sol scores 16.2 points higher than GPT-5.5. OpenAI believes the "automated researcher" is within reach....

items.title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
items.summary: A new analysis from OpenAI reveals issues in SWE-Bench Pro, a popular coding benchmark, raising concerns about reliability and accuracy in evaluating AI models.

items.title: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
items.summary: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.

items.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
items.summary: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production.

items.title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
items.summary: Tin AI từ Google DeepMind về "Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership". Mở bài gốc để đọc chi tiết và đánh giá tác động với team.

items.title: OpenAI kills its Atlas browser after just eight months and folds everything into ChatGPT
items.summary: OpenAI is killing its AI browser Atlas less than eight months after launch. Its features are moving into ChatGPT's updated Chrome extension, which will let users run ChatGPT directly in Chrome's sidebar. Atlas joins a growing list of scrapped OpenAI products. The article OpenAI kills its Atlas browser after just eight months and folds everything into ChatGPT appeared first on The Decoder .

items.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
items.summary: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."
